beats.narration: Eddy currents need room — a footprint about one coil wide, three skin depths deep. The moment shape intrudes, the signal shifts.

payload.title: GEOMETRICAL EFFECTS
payload.sub: Shape moves the signal — no flaw required.

beats.narration: Approach an edge and the loops compress against the boundary — current can't cross into air. That response starts a probe diameter out, burying cracks.

payload.heading: Edge effect: the loudest signal
payload.note: LOOPS COMPRESS AT THE BOUNDARY · BEGINS ~1 PROBE DIAMETER OUT

beats.narration: Shape speaks four ways. Edges crowd the loops. A thin wall lets the back surface answer. Curvature varies coupling. Holes are circular edges.

tiles.s: An edge wrapped in a circle

beats.narration: The instrument can't tell shape from a crack — both move the impedance. But geometry follows the drawing, repeating at the same place every time.
So make geometry constant. Calibrate on matching standards, scan parallel to edges at a fixed offset, and flag any break in the pattern.

payload.sub: Constant geometry can be balanced out — a break is earned.
